visualization.title: Number of courses: no prerequisites vs at least one

resultRaw.sql:
WITH course_pr_counts AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    COUNT(pr.id) AS prereq_count
  FROM public.courses AS c
  LEFT JOIN public.prerequisites AS pr
    ON pr.course_id = c.id
  GROUP BY c.id
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cpc.prereq_count = 0 THEN 'no_prerequisites' ELSE 'at_least_one' END AS prereq_category,
  COUNT(cpc.course_id) AS course_count
FROM course_pr_counts AS cpc
GROUP BY CASE WHEN cpc.prereq_count = 0 THEN 'no_prerequisites' ELSE 'at_least_one' END
ORDER BY MIN(cpc.prereq_count) ASC;

issues: SQL uses COUNT(cpc.course_id) instead of COUNT(DISTINCT public.courses.id) — functionally equivalent here because the CTE has one row per course, but differs from the stated alias in the plan.
ORDER BY MIN(cpc.prereq_count) is valid to sort categories (0 before >0) but is a slightly indirect way to order; explicit category sort or ORDER BY prereq_category might be clearer.
X-axis title in the visualization is 'Prerequisite category' (readable) while the plan used the field name 'prereq_category' — purely cosmetic, not a functional mismatch.

visualization.title: Number of courses: no prerequisites vs at least one

result.sql:
WITH course_pr_counts AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    COUNT(pr.id) AS prereq_count
  FROM public.courses AS c
  LEFT JOIN public.prerequisites AS pr
    ON pr.course_id = c.id
  GROUP BY c.id
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cpc.prereq_count = 0 THEN 'no_prerequisites' ELSE 'at_least_one' END AS prereq_category,
  COUNT(cpc.course_id) AS course_count
FROM course_pr_counts AS cpc
GROUP BY CASE WHEN cpc.prereq_count = 0 THEN 'no_prerequisites' ELSE 'at_least_one' END
ORDER BY MIN(cpc.prereq_count) ASC;

issues: SQL uses COUNT(cpc.course_id) instead of COUNT(DISTINCT public.courses.id) — functionally equivalent here because the CTE has one row per course, but differs from the stated alias in the plan.
ORDER BY MIN(cpc.prereq_count) is valid to sort categories (0 before >0) but is a slightly indirect way to order; explicit category sort or ORDER BY prereq_category might be clearer.
X-axis title in the visualization is 'Prerequisite category' (readable) while the plan used the field name 'prereq_category' — purely cosmetic, not a functional mismatch.
